summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates using stock screeners (Charles Schwab) to identify volatile stocks suitable for covered call selling. He filters by sector (Information Technology), price range ($20-60), optionability, short interest (10-20%), and beta (1-2) to narrow candidates from 1,200 stocks to 9, then shows how to evaluate premium income on specific strikes.
takeaways: Filter stocks by sector and price range to reduce screener results (1,200 → 158)
Use beta of 1-2 to identify appropriate volatility for covered calls
Target short interest 10-20% to avoid excessive short squeeze risk
Sell weekly options expiring Friday for covered calls
Go slightly out-of-the-money (e.g., $0.26-0.76 above current price) to capture $120-140 premium per contract on $5,000 position
High volatility sectors (AI, cybersecurity, healthcare) offer better premiums than consumer staples
Kenneth generates ~$600/week from covered call premium selling
Verify optionability filter to ensure stocks support options trading

strategy_candidates.change: Apply screener filters (beta 1-2, short interest 10-20%, tech sector, price $20-60) to identify covered call candidates weekly to systematically replace underperforming positions in the $50k portfolio
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates precise screener methodology reducing 1,200 candidates to 9 qualified stocks; applying this systematically would support Brian's $1,250/month premium target by ensuring higher-quality entry points and consistent weekly income generation

title: How to use Screeners for Covered Calls
